page: Amazon Web Services c7i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded compression tests placing in the top 25% tier. Text processing performance is highly competitive, reaching the top 10% tier. Multi-core execution, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks, falls within the average range. However, memory performance presents a tradeoff; while cached small-block reads are fast, the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%, and large-block uncached memory operations remain average.

The c7i.2xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from advanced vector and matrix extensions, such as AVX-512 and AMX. Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage, it is less suitable for memory-bound databases or large-scale LLM text generation, where its 7B model inference speed is relatively weak. Qualitatively, it offers a cost-efficient profile for CPU-bound tasks that require dedicated compute resources without the overhead of high memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, though multi-core performance and memory benchmarks remain average to weak. The instance is well-suited for compute-bound workloads such as web serving, serialization, and batch processing where high memory density is not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline, designed for CPU-intensive workloads.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced Intel Xeon processing for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of DDR5 RAM.
Strong single-core performance in text processing and single-threaded data compression.
Equipped with advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX.
Features a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware.
Best suited for web serving, batch processing, and compute-heavy applications.
